patch.value: Extension activation: no teardown/deactivation hook — extensions cannot clean up timers, connections, or state

Extensions can register hooks and overrides during activate(api), but there is no corresponding deactivate() or onTeardown() lifecycle. An extension that opens a database connection, starts a timer, or allocates a buffer at activation has no way to clean up when the CLI process exits or when 'pm extension reload' is called. This matters most for: (1) packages running long search-index connections (pm-search-advanced with an external vector store), (2) lifecycle-hooks that hold open log sinks, (3) reload scenarios in MCP where extensions are re-loaded per request. An optional `deactivate(): Promise<void> | void` export on the extension module (analogous to VS Code's deactivate export) would close this gap.

- Extension API supports a deactivate/teardown lifecycle hook invoked on shutdown/reload
- Long-running MCP server invokes teardown on extension reload
- Covered by tests; lifecycle documented
